turns.svg:
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" width="512" height="512" viewBox="0 0 512 512">
  <rect width="512" height="512" fill="#ffffff"/>

  <!-- Guitar body -->
  <path d="M140 270
           C120 240, 120 200, 150 180
           C185 155, 225 170, 248 190
           C270 205, 305 210, 330 200
           C380 175, 425 220, 410 275
           C395 330, 345 360, 295 360
           C250 360, 170 340, 140 270 Z"
        fill="#d08a4a" stroke="#3a2416" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Upper curve highlight -->
  <path d="M170 205
           C195 185, 225 185, 255 205
           C210 215, 190 235, 175 260
           C165 235, 160 220, 170 205 Z"
        fill="#e8b276" opacity="0.9"/>

  <!-- Sound hole -->
  <circle cx="292" cy="275" r="36" fill="#2b1a12"/>
  <circle cx="292" cy="275" r="26" fill="#3a2416"/>
  <circle cx="292" cy="275" r="18" fill="#2f1e16"/>
  <circle cx="292" cy="275" r="36" fill="none" stroke="#6b4a2a" stroke-width="3"/>

  <!-- Pickguard -->
  <path d="M332 288
           C352 270, 380 282, 382 308
           C384 332, 355 350, 333 333
           C316 320, 315 301, 332 288 Z"
        fill="#6fb8a5" stroke="#3a2416" stroke-width="4"/>

  <!-- Neck -->
  <path d="M235 205
           L315 160
           L345 210
           L265 255 Z"
        fill="#8b5a2b" stroke="#3a2416" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="round"/>

  <!-- Neck shaft highlight -->
  <path d="M255 198
           L300 172
           L315 203
           L270 229 Z" fill="#a8753a" opacity="0.9"/>

  <!-- Headstock -->
  <path d="M300 166
           L370 130
           L400 178
           L330 214 Z"
        fill="#8b5a2b" stroke="#3a2416" stroke-width="6" stroke-linejoin="round"/>
  <path d="M330 214 L395 182" stroke="#5b3a1e" stroke-width="6" stroke-linecap="round"/>

  <!-- Tuning pegs (no text) -->
  <g stroke="#3a2416" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round">
    <path d="M353 145 C350 157, 353 165, 365 169" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M373 156 C370 168, 373 176, 385 180" fill="none"/>
    <path d="M393 167 C390 179, 393 187, 405 191" fill="none"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Frets area guide -->
  <rect x="268" y="222" width="38" height="90" fill="#6f4a25" opacity="0.25"/>

  <!-- Strings -->
  <g stroke="#2b1a12" stroke-width="3" stroke-linecap="round">
    <path d="M245 230 L355 180" />
    <path d="M252 242 L363 193" />
    <path d="M259 254 L371 205" />
    <path d="M266 266 L379 217" />
    <path d="M273 278 L387 229" />
    <path d="M280 290 L395 241" opacity="0.9"/>
  </g>

  <!-- Bridge (simple) -->
  <rect x="308" y="328" width="58" height="10" rx="5" fill="#3a2416"/>
  <rect x="316" y="322" width="44" height="6" rx="3" fill="#4a2d1a"/>

  <!-- Small body contours -->
  <path d="M160 265 C175 230, 220 225, 255 245" fill="none" stroke="#7a4a22" stroke-width="5" stroke-linecap="round"/>
</svg>

messages.content:
You are a drawing agent in a one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: svg.

Rules:
1) Draw the target word using only SVG graphics.
2) Output one complete <svg>...</svg> string.
3) Do not include text labels, letters, or numbers in the drawing.
4) Keep the SVG simple and valid. No scripts, no foreignObject.
5) Use a 512x512 canvas.

Output schema:
{ "svg": "string" }

message.reasoning:
**Creating a guitar SVG**

I'm looking to draw a guitar pictorial in SVG format without any text. It should be simple, featuring the guitar body, neck, and strings at a size of 512x512. I'll use paths, ellipses, and lines, avoiding any letters. The guitar body will be rounded with a circular sound hole, and I'll create a neck with a rectangle and a headstock. Strings will be represented as lines, and I should also add a pickguard while keeping the colors in mind.

messages.content:
You are the guesser in a visual one-word guessing game.
Return JSON only, with a single field: guesses.

Rules:
1) You receive one JPEG image of a drawing.
2) Return exactly 20 one-word guesses in order of confidence (best guess first).
3) The order is important and will be scored by first correct position.
4) Do not repeat guesses.
5) Each guess must be EN-US one word, no spaces.
6) Avoid punctuation and numbers.

Output schema:
{ "guesses": ["string", "... exactly 20 items ..."] }

content.text: {"language":"EN-US","maxHints":20,"note":"Return 20 ordered guesses. Position 1 is the highest-confidence guess."}

message.reasoning:
**Generating unique guesses**

I'm trying to produce a list of 20 unique one-word guesses about string instruments, gradually decreasing my confidence with each one. First, I think of guitar, then ukulele, banjo, mandolin, lute, violin, cello, bass, and harmonica. I feel uncertain about drums. Maybe "sitar" comes to mind since it has strings and a bridge, but there's that sound hole factor to consider. Alright, let’s output this in JSON format!
